basics.name: Miguel Garcia
basics.label: Production Engineer
basics.summary: Software engineer with experience across distributed systems, production engineering, developer infrastructure, and machine learning. I enjoy improving reliability, performance, and observability in systems that operate at scale.

work.name: Meta - Messaging Infrastructure
highlights: Improved read and write performance of a distributed message storage system with cache preloading and reduced redundant data fetches, lowering read latency by 50% and increasing write throughput by 40%
Built a load testing framework ahead of a large-scale data migration, uncovering throughput risks that influenced migration strategy
Added observability across critical data flows, enabling detection of multiple production incidents and reducing client-side error rates by 20%
Designed dashboards, alerts, and debugging runbooks that became the team’s operational source of truth
Served as primary oncall engineer for a Tier 0 messaging storage service and documented root causes for high-severity incidents

work.name: Goldman Sachs - DevOps/Runtime Engineering
highlights: Managed deployments and maintained infrastructure for a consumer-facing financial services platform
Built dashboards for real-time visibility into system performance and operational metrics
Debugged CI/CD pipeline issues to improve deployment reliability and reduce release failures

work.name: Goldman Sachs - DevOps/Runtime Engineering
highlights: Supported deployments and internal infrastructure for runtime engineering workflows
Improved operational practices and automation for a more reliable delivery process

work.name: Texas A&M University
highlights: Taught data structures, algorithms, and Python fundamentals across CS and engineering courses
Held weekly office hours supporting 100+ students

education.institution: Texas A&M University, College Station, Texas
courses: Coursework included data structures, algorithms, machine learning, AI, and networking systems

awards.title: Distinguished Student Award
awards.summary: Recognized for academic achievement during Spring 2022 and Spring 2023.

projects.name: Zenji - Kanji Learning App
projects.summary: Created and trained a convolutional neural network for recognizing and providing feedback on kanji characters, then helped deliver the experience as a Progressive Web App.
highlights: TypeScript
Firebase
TensorFlow

projects.name: ICU Mortality Prediction Model
projects.summary: Built a mortality prediction model on ICU patient data, compared several architectures, and selected the strongest performer based on evaluation metrics after hyperparameter tuning.
highlights: Python
Pandas
PyTorch

projects.name: KANM Student Radio Website
projects.summary: Collaborated with a student team to build a working UI for the A&M student radio website and developed frontend components informed by Figma-driven design work.
highlights: React.js
Frontend Development
UI Design
